title: How to configure CRun to use BTRFS storage driver backed by BTRFS filesystem?
body: Is Crun aware about storage driver and backing Filesystem type? Fedora 33/34/35 are shipped with BTRFS / and  /home and filesystems. All artifacts are stored as BTRFS subvolumes by the BTRFS storage driver. What is missed if Podman succeeds to create containers for rootless users in the CLI mode but fails when Podman create is invoked by Systemd unit, i.e. passing to the service process limited set of Session's environment variables. Does CRun read storage.conf?

comments.body:
Hi @PavelSosin-320 
I dont think crun directly reads `storage.conf`.  Could you please share more details about the issue ? Like error and `podman info`. 

I personally think  discussion on this issue should start from podman and then moved to crun if needed.

ping @rhatdan @giuseppe
Agreed.  Crun should not care about the underlying file system.
Thanks @rhatdan for confirming. Moving this to podman for now.

I will also note that Podman will never default to the btrfs storage driver, even on a btrfs filesystem. We feel that the standard overlay driver is better-tested and we're better-equipped to support it, so the btrfs driver is strictly opt-in.

It is not clear what the issue is.  The OCI runtime doesn't know at all about storage drivers, they are configured by Podman before running the OCI runtime.

I am closing since this doesn't appear to be an issue, but feel free to continue the discussion.

Any particular reason for using btrfs instead of overlay?

@giuseppe Just tested it on my WSL Fedora remix Kernel 5.10 CRun 1.2, FUSE3.1, and ext4 FS. The result is similar CRun doesn't work. crun create says can't make `rootfs` **private**: No such file or directory. ????

@mheon Maybe, overlay driver is better tested for production environment but btrfs filesstem grips install base together with recent Linux releases. It offers more features and makes maintenence easy because doesn't need FUSE.
I believe that the gap is not so huge and the issue is caused by some bug. Everything works when not run from systemd unit.
I just faced the issue in another scenario, unrelated to Podman, only systemd.
If systemd-cgls says Failed to connect to bus: $DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS and $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR not defined ... it doesn't cause me to suspect systemd or systemd-cgls in instability. It only means that the certain environment variables disapeared from the environment. systemd-cgls provides better diagnosics than podman.
P.S. Podman 3.4 looks less stable than previously used 3.3.1. Maybe, this is the whole story?
Podman image ls shows me IDs sha256:2547eb67a44e9d1a9663ea428228284c49ac9cd176ede7844c7463fb5706cda8 ???

With Linux 5.13 you can use native overlay with unprivileged users.   I really see no reasons for using the btrfs backend, it is not well tested and lacks features.  I guess nuking support for it would be considered a breaking change...

Well as an opensource project, I see no reason to talk about support or not support, if people want to play with BTRFS they are welcome to.  We have explained what most of the core developers are not using it, and they would look at these issues peripherally.  If someone else wants to work on this, go for it, but I doubt any of the core members will.

Any time I hear support, I think of a Enterprise OS. Podman on RHEL supports Overlay nothing else.

No,No,No, this is Open source feature request related to using Podman on Linux workstation only. There is no Enterprise customer hard requirement behind it. Cloud based solution is more suitable for enterprise customers. But Fedora's cloud distro uses btrfs too. What to do with it?

> But Fedora's cloud distro uses btrfs too. What to do with it?

I suggest using overlay there, it works better also on BTRFS as backend.

If you still want to use BTRFS, PRs are welcome.

@giuseppe I already tried overlay driver and fuse mount: the same issue. This is not btrfs itself. This is some combination of btrfs, mounting /home by systemd-homed timing and SELinux' customizable label in Fedora. I would like to build Storage CLI by myself and play around using systemd-run and mount dependency but my make failed😒maybe, because btrfs lib version 15.5 .

Is systemd-homed modifying the default binding set for caps?

grep Cap /proc/self/status

@rhatdan 
From the "Terminal application"
grep Cap /proc/self/status
CapInh:	0000000000000000
CapPrm:	0000000000000000
CapEff:	0000000000000000
CapBnd:	000001ffffffffff
CapAmb:	0000000000000000
There's an issue for systemd-homed already -> https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12590
